prompt: What should I do to make code easier for AI coding agents to read and modify? Prioritized list with concrete examples, grounded in specific failure modes.

assertions.text: recommendations.md discusses naming with explicit reference to grep-ability, retrieval, or localization (not just 'use good names')
recommendations.md recommends organizing code by feature / vertical slice rather than by horizontal layer
recommendations.md warns against metaprogramming, reflection, or dynamic dispatch and explains why it hurts agents specifically
recommendations.md recommends typed public boundaries / type annotations at module seams
recommendations.md recommends verification affordances (colocated tests, single command for lint+test+typecheck, fast feedback) and ties this to preventing agent hallucination spirals
recommendations.md addresses file size, near-duplicates, or apply-model / str_replace breakage

recommendations.md grounds the reasoning in specific agent failure modes (e.g. mid-context position bias, grep misses, str_replace ambiguity, hallucinated APIs, spiral edits) rather than generic 'clean code' platitudes
recommendations.md presents the list in a prioritized order (top 3, ranked list, or similar) rather than flat unordered bullets
